tools.content: Use the bounded filters to inspect selected USDA Market News observations. Always read the unit basis and report level before comparing values. The release makes no retail-price or availability claim.

chart.accessibleSummary: The selected wholesale observations and their exact unit basis are available in the accessible table.

definition.limitation: Market News basis, inventory, and shipping-point observations describe wholesale or first-handler context. They are not retail shelf prices or store availability.

table.caption: Selected USDA Market News wholesale observations

tools.description: Browse selected USDA Market News observations with commodity, geography, period, unit basis, narrative context, and the wholesale boundary kept prominent.

rows.narrative: Cream availability was lighter in parts of the Central region; the published range describes first sales F.O.B. and is not a retail shelf price.
rows.title: Butter - Central U.S.

tools.content: Use the bounded controls to narrow the approved static release. Electricity and natural gas use different units, so compare values only within the same series and unit. The full source table remains visible without JavaScript.

chart.accessibleSummary: A static trend plot of the selected EIA aggregate; the complete values remain available in the table.

definition.limitation: These are EIA residential aggregates for the named geography and period. They are not a utility tariff, an individual household bill, or a prediction.

tools.description: Compare approved state and United States residential electricity or natural-gas aggregates by period, with units and limitations kept visible.

social.description: Explore static EIA residential energy aggregates by geography, series, and period without mistaking them for an individual bill.
social.title: Household Energy Benchmark Explorer

tools.title: Household Energy Benchmark Explorer

tools.content: Filter the approved static rows by commodity, status, or year. The table retains units and source-load context, including forecast and final classification, and remains complete without JavaScript.

chart.accessibleSummary: A static national production trend; exact values, units, and final or forecast classification remain in the table.

definition.limitation: National crop production does not imply local store inventory, availability, quality, or price. Forecast rows must not be presented as final estimates.

table.caption: Approved USDA NASS national crop production statistics

tools.description: Compare approved national crop production statistics by commodity and year while keeping final, forecast, unit, revision, and suppression states visible.

social.description: Explore approved NASS production rows with commodity, period, unit, final or forecast classification, and revision context.
social.title: U.S. Crop Production Trend Explorer

tools.title: U.S. Crop Production Trend Explorer

tools.content: These are stable food identities, not silently inferred retail products. The current broker output did not expose complete nutrient names and units, so the release correctly publishes no nutrient amounts.

chart.accessibleSummary: The approved food identity records are provided as a complete accessible table.

inputs.label: Approved food identity
options: Beans, black turtle, mature seeds, canned
Cereals, oats, regular and quick, not fortified, dry
Leavening agents, baking powder, double-acting, straight phosphate
Milk, whole, 3.25% milkfat, with added vitamin D
Rice, white, long-grain, regular, raw, enriched
Tomatoes, red, ripe, canned, packed in tomato juice

definition.limitation: FoodData identity is not a store listing, current price, or availability claim. Nutrients appear only when name, amount, unit, and basis are all present.

table.caption: Approved USDA FoodData Central staple identities

tools.content: Select only from ZIP codes already present in the approved static release. Residential weights show why a single-label answer can be misleading. Nothing entered here is sent to a provider or retained.

chart.accessibleSummary: The complete weighted relationship data is presented in the accessible table.

definition.limitation: A postal ZIP is not a neighborhood, and a ZIP can relate to multiple counties. ZIP relationships also differ from Census ZCTA approximations.

table.caption: HUD USPS ZIP Code Crosswalk relationships

tools.description: Inspect approved HUD ZIP-to-county and ZIP-to-CBSA relationships, including residential weights and honest ambiguity instead of neighborhood guesses.

social.description: See bounded HUD county and metro relationships with residential weights and an explicit explanation of ambiguous ZIP codes.
